content.text: The bug is a cache-key collision. The key is built only from `opts.team`, ignoring `includeArchived`. So when something calls `getUsers({team, includeArchived: true})`, the archived-inclusive result gets cached under `users:<team>`, and a later `getUsers({team, includeArchived: false})` gets that same cached blob back — archived users leak in. After 60s the cache entry expires, which is why refreshing after a minute clears it.

content.text:
Fixed. All 3 tests pass.

## What was wrong

`getUsers` in `src/userService.js` built its cache key from only the team:

```js
const key = `users:${opts.team}`;
```

But results depend on `opts.includeArchived` too. So a call for archived-inclusive results (`includeArchived: true`) cached the full list under `users:<team>`, and a later active-only call (`includeArchived: false`) hit that same key and got archived users back — even though it never requested them. The 60s TTL is exactly why refreshing after a minute clears it: the poisoned entry expires and the next fetch is clean.

## The fix

Include `includeArchived` in the cache key so the two variants can't collide:

```js
const key = `users:${opts.team}:${opts.includeArchived ? "all" : "active"}`;
```

I also added `test/userService.test.js` (there was none) with a regression test for the leak plus one confirming identical requests still hit the cache.

result:
Fixed. All 3 tests pass.

## What was wrong

`getUsers` in `src/userService.js` built its cache key from only the team:

```js
const key = `users:${opts.team}`;
```

But results depend on `opts.includeArchived` too. So a call for archived-inclusive results (`includeArchived: true`) cached the full list under `users:<team>`, and a later active-only call (`includeArchived: false`) hit that same key and got archived users back — even though it never requested them. The 60s TTL is exactly why refreshing after a minute clears it: the poisoned entry expires and the next fetch is clean.

## The fix

Include `includeArchived` in the cache key so the two variants can't collide:

```js
const key = `users:${opts.team}:${opts.includeArchived ? "all" : "active"}`;
```

I also added `test/userService.test.js` (there was none) with a regression test for the leak plus one confirming identical requests still hit the cache.
